mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth indicates a healthy expansion in aggregate demand and corporate earnings, which supports higher equity valuations over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical correlation between GDP growth and SPX 1-month forward returns is positive (0.3) over past 20 years
failure_condition: If the average 20-day SPX forward return following quarters with positive GDP growth is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05 over a 10-year rolling window), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labour market weakness and impending recession, which historically leads to negative equity returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Sahm rule trigger historically associated with recession onset
failure_condition: If after each trigger the average 20-day SPX return is not negative or the proportion of negative returns is less than 55%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is contracting (below its 6-month moving average) while the federal funds rate remains elevated above 5%, it indicates stagflationary conditions that compress profit margins and discount rates negatively, leading to poor equity returns over a quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflationary periods in 1970s saw negative real equity returns
failure_condition: If over any 5-year period, the 60-day forward return when joint condition holds is not negative or not significantly lower than unconditional mean (rank sum test p>0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: During NBER-defined expansion phases, increasing housing starts reflect strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which boosts economic momentum and supports positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts lead real GDP growth by 2-3 quarters
failure_condition: If during NBER expansion months, the 60-day forward return following positive month-over-month HOUST growth is not positive on average over a 20-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail and food services sales exceeding 0.5% signals an abrupt consumer spending slowdown, which typically foreshadows lower corporate revenues and negative equity returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending contractions are leading indicators of earnings recessions
failure_condition: If after each trigger the average 20-day SPX return is not negative or the win rate of negative returns is below 60% over at least 10 occurrences, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in payroll employment growth (below 100K per month over 3 months) and a decline in industrial production over two consecutive months signals a broad-based economic contraction, leading to negative equity returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS growth < 100K and 2-month INDPRO decline
evidence_refs: fact:Coincident indicators declining together predict recessions with high accuracy
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields 60-day positive returns in more than 30% of instances or average return is not negative at 95% confidence over a 15-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production, measured by positive month-over-month growth, indicates robust manufacturing activity and rising capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive short-term equity returns over a weekly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:IP surprises have a positive immediate impact on equity markets
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return following positive IP growth is not positive at a statistically significant level over a 10-year rolling window (t-test p<0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: During a Federal Reserve monetary easing cycle, a decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity cash flows, increasing present values and driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2000, equity markets rally in the 3 months following initial rate cuts
failure_condition: If during NBER-defined easing cycles (FOMC rate cuts), the 60-day forward SPX return is not positive on average over at least 5 distinct cycles, reject.
